{{Quote|He followed the Seps down. Focused on bringing back tangible proof of the 1138 and the Seps' intent. Got to respect that.|CC-5052, regarding Rii'ke En|<ref name="SWR 68" />}}
'''Rii'ke En''' was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] who served the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] as a [[Jedi General/Legends|general]] during the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]]. In [[20 BBY/Legends|20 BBY]], En and the [[Rii'ke En's cruiser|cruiser]] under his command [[Battle of Honoghr/Legends|engaged]] the [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Separatist]] [[Core ship/Legends|cruiser]] ''[[Gahenna]]'' over the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Honoghr/Legends|Honoghr]]. The ''Gahenna'' was transporting a defoliant [[Poison/Legends|toxin]], [[Trihexalophine1138]], which the Separatists intended to use on the planet [[Naboo/Legends|Naboo]]. When the Separatist [[Starship/Legends|ship]] crashed on Honoghr, En and a [[Unidentified clone trooper (Honoghr)|clone trooper]] under his command traveled to the surface to find the [[Scientific Instrument Package]], which had data on the virus. En tracked the SIP to an ancient [[Rakata/Legends|Rakatan]] [[Honoghr Rakatan temple|temple]], where the local [[Noghri/Legends|Noghri]] had taken it. After infiltrating the temple, En sent a report to [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] Knight [[Aayla Secura/Legends|Aayla Secura]]. However, he and the [[Clone trooper/Legends|clone trooper]] accompanying him were attacked by the Noghri protecting the temple, resulting in both of their [[Death/Legends|deaths]].

==Biography==
{{Quote|General En followed them into the temple and sent a report telling us where the SIP was taken. My General was on the comm with En at that time. My unit was among those responding. En was ordered to keep out of sight until a team could link up with him—the Noghri had already killed every other Rep or Sep that had made down to the planet. En managed to get word that he'd already been spotted. Transmission ended there.|CC-5052, regarding Rii'ke En|<ref name="SWR 68" />}}
Rii'ke En, a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]], was born during the final [[Time/Legends|decades]] of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]]. He was [[Jedi training/Legends|trained]] in the ways of [[The Force/Legends|the Force]] by the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]] and eventually attained the rank of [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]].<ref name="SWR 68" /> When the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]] [[First Battle of Geonosis/Legends|began]] in [[22 BBY/Legends|22 BBY]],<ref name="Star Wars: Episode II Attack of the Clones">{{Film|II}}</ref> En served the [[Grand Army of the Republic/Legends|Grand Army of the Republic]] as a [[Jedi General/Legends|Jedi General]].<ref name="SWR 68" />

[[File:RiikeEnDeath-Republic68.jpg|thumb|left|200px|Rii'ke En is overwhelmed and killed by the Noghri guards in Honoghr's Rakatan temple.]]
In [[20 BBY/Legends|20 BBY]], En and the [[Rii'ke En's cruiser|cruiser]] that he commanded [[Battle of Honoghr/Legends|fought]] a [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Separatist]] [[Core ship/Legends|core ship]], the ''[[Gahenna]]'',<ref name="NEC: DOH" /> over the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Honoghr/Legends|Honoghr]]. The ''Gahenna'' was transporting the defoliant [[Poison/Legends|toxin]] [[Trihexalophine1138]] for the Separatists' use against the planet [[Naboo/Legends|Naboo]]. When the battle-damaged Confederate [[Starship/Legends|ship]] crashed on the surface of Honoghr, En took [[Unidentified clone trooper (Honoghr)|one]] [[Clone trooper/Legends|clone trooper]] with him to locate the [[Scientific Instrument Package]], which contained both evidence pertaining to the virus and proof of the Separatists' intentions with it.<ref name="SWR 68" />

After three days, En and the [[Trooper/Legends|trooper]] reached the ''Gahenna''{{'s}} crash site and discovered that the native [[Noghri/Legends|Noghri]] had taken the SIP when they salvaged the ship. En managed to track the SIP to an ancient [[Rakata/Legends|Rakatan]] [[Honoghr Rakatan temple|temple]], where the Noghri had taken the package. The [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] and the [[Cloning/Legends|clone]] trooper infiltrated the temple, and En sent a report via [[Comlink/Legends|comlink]] to Jedi [[General/Legends|General]] [[Aayla Secura/Legends|Aayla Secura]] informing her of the SIP's location. Secura ordered En to refrain from obtaining the device until a back-up [[Squad/Legends|squad]] reached their location. However, the Noghri guarding the temple were alerted to the presence of En and the clone. Letting Secura know he had been detected, En engaged the attacking Noghri, who had already [[Death/Legends|killed]] the trooper. Shortly after, En was overwhelmed and killed by the Noghri. Secura managed to eventually complete En's mission by recovering the SIP for the Republic,<ref name="SWR 68" /> although Honoghr's ecosystem was eventually devastated by the Trihexalophine1138 toxin.<ref name="NEC: DOH" />

==Personality and traits==
The Human male Rii'ke En wore a set of [[Color/Legends|brown]] [[Jedi apparel/Legends|Jedi robes]] and owned a [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]] containing a [[Color/Legends|green]] [[Lightsaber crystal/Legends|crystal]]. He was determined to find the SIP, which would prove the existence of the 1138 virus and the Separatists' intentions for it. Even after the clone trooper accompanying him was killed, En still fought the Noghri defending the temple until he himself was killed.<ref name="SWR 68" />

==Powers and abilities==
En was [[Force-sensitive/Legends|sensitive]] to the Force, and used a lightsaber during battle. However, his skills were not enough to prevent him from being overrun by the Noghri in Honoghr's Rakatan temple.<ref name="SWR 68" />

==Behind the scenes==
Rii'ke En first appeared in [[Republic 68|''Republic'' 68]], the sixty-eighth issue of the ''[[Star Wars: Republic]]'' [[Star Wars comic books|comic book]] series. The issue was written by [[John Ostrander]], illustrated by [[Jan Duursema]],<ref name="SWR 68" /> and released on [[August 25]], [[2004]].<ref name="Dark Horse">{{DarkHorse|url=Comics/13-082/Star-Wars-Republic-68|text=Star Wars: Republic #68}}</ref>
